mechanism_narrative: A persistently elevated federal funds rate raises the risk-free discount rate applied to future corporate cash flows, mechanically lowering present equity values and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Positive 20-day S&P 500 returns occur in more than 55% of observations during the highest FEDFUNDS quartile over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.3 percentage points over two months signals deteriorating household income and collapsing aggregate demand, triggering de-risking and negative S&P 500 returns over the following quarter.
failure_condition: S&P 500 posts positive 60-day returns in greater than 50% of instances when the UNRATE trigger fires during late-cycle expansions.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month growth in retail sales and industrial production indicates a broad-based demand expansion that reduces recession probability, lifting short-term risk appetite and generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next week.
failure_condition: The joint positive signal from RSAFS and INDPRO does not produce positive 5-day returns in at least 55% of joint up-months over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate exceeds 4.5%, elevated CPIAUCSL inflation signals that the Federal Reserve will maintain restrictive policy for longer, tightening financial conditions and producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: S&P 500 generates positive 20-day returns in more than 50% of high-rate high-inflation observations over a 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Two consecutive monthly declines in industrial production signal contracting manufacturing capacity utilization and falling corporate earnings expectations, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Negative 60-day S&P 500 returns occur in fewer than 50% of triggered instances across a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A decline in housing starts below 1400 thousand units indicates weakening construction activity and reduced forward-looking household wealth effects, compressing cyclical equity exposure and producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: S&P 500 posts positive 20-day returns in more than 55% of observations when HOUST is below 1400 over a 2-year rolling period.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent acceleration in nonfarm payrolls and a decline in the unemployment rate signal a tightening labor market that boosts consumer spending power and corporate revenue visibility, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next week.
failure_condition: The interaction between PAYEMS growth and UNRATE declines fails to produce positive 5-day returns in at least 55% of joint signal months over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month acceleration in industrial production signals improving manufacturing momentum and operating leverage, which drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next week as earnings revisions turn positive.
failure_condition: Positive 5-day returns occur in fewer than 55% of months when INDPRO accelerates over a 2-year rolling window.
